    <style>
/* ==========================================================================
   THE 418 CHALLENGE: THEME-AWARE RETRO EDITION
   ========================================================================== */

.articletag-418challenge {
  font-family: "Comic Sans MS", "Chalkboard SE", "Marker Felt", sans-serif !important;
  cursor: crosshair !important;
}

/* Destroy modern rounded corners and add brutal borders */
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-card,
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__main,
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__header {
  border-radius: 0 !important;
  margin-bottom: 2rem !important;
}

/* Typography specifics */
.articletag-418challenge h1,
.articletag-418challenge h2,
.articletag-418challenge h3 {
  text-transform: uppercase !important;
}

/* Fake <marquee> effect for the main article title */
.articletag-418challenge h1.fs-3xl {
  animation: retro-slide 5s linear infinite alternate;
  overflow: visible;
}

/* Spin those avatars */
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__header__meta img {
  border-radius: 0 !important; 
  animation: retro-spin 5s linear infinite;
}

/* Obnoxious tags */
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-tag {
  border-radius: 0 !important;
  font-weight: 900 !important;
  transform: rotate(-3deg);
  display: inline-block;
}
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-tag:nth-child(even) {
  transform: rotate(3deg);
}

/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   2. LIGHT THEME (Windows 95 / Office 97 Vibes)
   -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge {
  background-color: #008080 !important; /* Win95 Teal Desktop */
}

body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge .crayons-card,
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__main,
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__header {
  background-color: #c0c0c0 !important; /* Classic dialog gray */
  color: #000000 !important;
  border: 4px outset #ffffff !important;
  box-shadow: 8px 8px 0px #000000 !important;
}

body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge h1,
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge h2,
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge h3 {
  color: #ff0000 !important;
  text-shadow: 2px 2px 0px #ffff00 !important;
  border-bottom: 3px dashed #0000ff !important;
}

body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge a,
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge .crayons-link {
  color: #0000ff !important; /* Standard unvisited blue */
  text-decoration: underline !important;
  font-weight: bold !important;
}

body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge a:hover,
body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge .crayons-link:hover {
  background-color: #ffff00 !important;
  color: #ff0000 !important;
}

body:not(.dark-theme) .articletag-418challenge .crayons-tag {
  background: #ffff00 !important;
  color: #ff0000 !important;
  border: 2px dotted #0000ff !important;
}

/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   3. DARK THEME (1999 Hacker / Deep GeoCities Vibes)
   -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge {
  background-color: #000000 !important;
  /* Dumb CSS: A scrolling, eye-bleeding neon green hacker grid with CRT scanlines */
  background-image: 
    linear-gradient(transparent 50%, rgba(0, 255, 0, 0.25) 50%), 
    linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(0, 255, 0, 0.5) 1px, transparent 1px), 
    linear-gradient(rgba(0, 255, 0, 0.5) 1px, transparent 1px) !important;
  background-size: 100% 4px, 20px 20px, 20px 20px !important;
  animation: retro-pan 60s linear infinite !important;
}

body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge .crayons-card,
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__main,
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge .crayons-article__header {
  background-color: #111111 !important;
  color: #00ff00 !important; /* Terminal Green */
  border: 4px outset #555555 !important;
  box-shadow: 8px 8px 0px #ff00ff !important; /* Hot pink shadow */
}

body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge h1,
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge h2,
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge h3 {
  color: #ff00ff !important; /* Magenta headers */
  text-shadow: 2px 2px 0px #00ffff !important; /* Cyan shadow */
  border-bottom: 3px dashed #00ff00 !important;
}

body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge a,
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge .crayons-link {
  color: #00ffff !important; /* Cyan links */
  text-decoration: underline !important;
  font-weight: bold !important;
}

body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge a:hover,
body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge .crayons-link:hover {
  background-color: #ff00ff !important;
  color: #ffffff !important;
}

body.dark-theme .articletag-418challenge .crayons-tag {
  background: #000000 !important;
  color: #00ff00 !important;
  border: 2px dotted #ff00ff !important;
}

/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   4. ANIMATIONS
   -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
@keyframes retro-spin {
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes retro-slide {
  0% { transform: translateX(-2%); }
  100% { transform: translateX(2%); }
}

@keyframes retro-pan {
  0% { background-position: 0 0, 0 0, 0 0; }
  /* Numbers must be multiples of the background-size (4px and 20px) to loop perfectly */
  100% { background-position: 0 100px, 40px 40px, 40px 40px; }
}

/* --------------------------------------------------------------------------
   5. BONUS: CAUTION TAPE REACTION BAR
   -------------------------------------------------------------------------- */
.articletag-418challenge .crayons-article-actions {
  background: repeating-linear-gradient(
    45deg,
    #ffff00,
    #ffff00 10px,
    #000000 10px,
    #000000 20px
  ) !important;
  border: 4px solid #ff0000 !important;
  border-radius: 0 !important;
}

.articletag-418challenge .crayons-article-actions button {
  background:black;
  color: #ff00ff !important;
}

.articletag-418challenge .crayons-article-actions .crayons-reaction__count {
    color: #ff00ff !important;
    font-weight: bold !important;
}

.articletag-418challenge .crayons-icon:not(.crayons-icon--default) * {
    fill: #ffff00 !important;
}

.articletag-418challenge .c-embed .crayons-btn--primary {
  color: #00ff00 !important;
}

.articletag-418challenge .popover-billboard {
margin-bottom: 0 !important;
}

.crayons-story__contentpreview .ltag__link--embedded {
margin-top: 0 !important;
}

.subscription-icon {
    max-height: 16px !important;
    display: inline-block !important;
}

@media screen and (min-width: 950px) {
    .event-show-layout {
        margin-top: 24px !important;
    }
}

@media screen and (max-width: 949px) {
    .event-show-layout {
        padding: 11px !important;
    }
}

Minimal Portfolio Examples for Web Developers Who Aren’t Good at Design","author":{"@context":"http://schema.org","@type":"Person","url":"https://dev.to/bookercodes","name":"Alex Booker"},"datePublished":"2022-06-29T17:50:18Z","dateModified":"2022-06-29T17:50:18Z","mainEntity":{"@type":"DiscussionForumPosting","@id":"#article-discussion-1127800","headline":"10 Minimal Portfolio Examples for Web Developers Who Aren’t Good at Design","text":"\u003cp\u003eNot all web developers are good at design, and that is OK! In the real world, you will often work with a designer on your team to convert their designs into real, responsive websites anyway.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eWith that said, to get a web developer job, you’ll need your portfolio to stand out and look good. Collaborating with a designer isn’t usually an option at this stage, so what do you do?\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eTemplates are great in a pinch, but it might be a bit awkward if two candidates apply with the same template 😬.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eThis  leaves the question as to what to do if you’re not keen on a generic template but don’t have much design practice either 🤔.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eThe trick is to \u003cstrong\u003ekeep it minimal and iterate!\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eRemember, the most important thing about your project portfolio is your projects.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eWhile the design should be pleasing, it should also get out of the way. You can always add pizzazz later 🕺🏻!\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eTo help you build a minimal, effective portfolio, we’ve curated 10 minimal web developer portfolio examples for you to learn from.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eLook out for the  🎨 design tips sections where we highlight some of the basic design principles you can specifically notice!\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eObviously, these creators wouldn’t like it very much if you copied their designs exactly (don’t do that 🙅🏻), but let them be your inspiration! It’s not uncommon for new creatives to mix and match their favorite 3-4 works to make something unique.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003ch2\u003e\n  \u003ca name=\"delba-de-oliveria\" href=\"#delba-de-oliveria\"\u003e\n  \u003c/a\u003e\n  Delba De Oliveria\n\u003c/h2\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F8931ekj88c4fn23282wh.gif\" class=\"article-body-image-wrapper\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F8931ekj88c4fn23282wh.gif\" alt=\"\" loading=\"lazy\" width=\"1284\" height=\"908\" data-animated=\"true\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eDelba’s project-based portfolio is remarkable. She even made a \u003ca href=\"https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=142\u0026amp;v=zl9iXZrw_dw\u0026amp;feature=youtu.be\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eYouTube video\u003c/a\u003e about it!\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eUnderstandably, Delba has since updated \u003ca href=\"https://delba.dev/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eher website\u003c/a\u003e to be more focused on her blog, so the live version looks a bit different today (still looks great.) Make sure to check out the original, minimal-looking version by flicking through the video.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e🎨 Design tip\u003c/strong\u003e:  Even though Delba’s website is fairly minimal, she sparks joy in the user with delightful highlighter animation using a JavaScript library called \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/rough-stuff/rough-notation\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eRough Notation\u003c/a\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003ch2\u003e\n  \u003ca name=\"alex-pate\" href=\"#alex-pate\"\u003e\n  \u003c/a\u003e\n  \u003ca href=\"https://alexjpate.com/\" target=\"_blank\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\"\u003eAlex Pate\u003c/a\u003e\n\u003c/h2\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fwjy6rjq4h51u69qjieth.png\" class=\"article-body-image-wrapper\"\u003e\u003cimg alt=\"Alex's portfolio in a desktop orientation\" loading=\"lazy\" width=\"800\" height=\"870\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\n\u003ca alt=\"Alex's portfolio in a mobile orientation\" loading=\"lazy\" width=\"800\" height=\"1364\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eNotice how Alex uses a fairly narrow single-column layout and lists only a few menu items at the top. This removed the need to collapse columns or introduce a hamburger menu on mobile, allowing Alex to ship his website sooner.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eThe \u003cstrong\u003eSelected Work\u003c/strong\u003e section, though minimal, is beautifully designed and effective:\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003e\u003ca href=\"https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fjbtggj8dt7c2e4grg247.png\" class=\"article-body-image-wrapper\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fjbtggj8dt7c2e4grg247.png\" alt=\"The Selected Work section of Alex's portfolio\" loading=\"lazy\" width=\"800\" height=\"768\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003eAlex understands someone looking at a portfolio is probably browsing and too many options is overwhelming. He makes it easy by showcasing only his 3 most recent works. For everything else, you can click \u003cstrong\u003eView All Work →\u003c/strong\u003e.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e🎨 Design tip\u003c/strong\u003e: Through introducing borders in the project screenshots, Alex introduced a pop of colour, while also making the design breathable.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003ch2\u003e\n  \u003ca name=\"lee-robinson\" href=\"#lee-robinson\"\u003e\n of your design skill.\u003c/p\u003e\n\n\u003ch2\u003e\n  \u003ca name=\"justin-chi\" href=\"#justin-chi\"\u003e\n  \u003c/a\u003e\n  \u003ca href=\"https://www.justinchi.me/\" target=\"_blank\"               <h1 class=" fs-3xl m:fs-4xl l:fs-5xl fw-bold s:fw-heavy lh-tight mb-2 long">
                10 Minimal Portfolio Examples for Web Developers Who Aren’t Good at Design
              </h1>
              
                  <div class="spec__tags flex flex-wrap">

        --tag-bg: rgba(247, 223, 30, 0.10);

          <div class="crayons-article__main ">
            <div class="crayons-article__body text-styles spec__body" data-article-id="1127800" id="article-body">
                <p>Not all web developers are good at design, and that is OK! In the real world, you will often work with a designer on your team to convert their designs into real, responsive websites anyway.</p>

<p>With that said, to get a web developer job, you’ll need your portfolio to stand out and look good. Collaborating with a designer isn’t usually an option at this stage, so what do you do?</p>

<p>Templates are great in a pinch, but it might be a bit awkward if two candidates apply with the same template 😬.</p>

<p>This  leaves the question as to what to do if you’re not keen on a generic template but don’t have much design practice either 🤔.</p>

<p>The trick is to <strong>keep it minimal and iterate!</strong></p>

<p>Remember, the most important thing about your project portfolio is your projects.</p>

<p>While the design should be pleasing, it should also get out of the way. You can always add pizzazz later 🕺🏻!</p>

<p>To help you build a minimal, effective portfolio, we’ve curated 10 minimal web developer portfolio examples for you to learn from.</p>

<p>Look out for the  🎨 design tips sections where we highlight some of the basic design principles you can specifically notice!</p>

<p>Obviously, these creators wouldn’t like it very much if you copied their designs exactly (don’t do that 🙅🏻), but let them be your inspiration! It’s not uncommon for new creatives to mix and match their favorite 3-4 works to make something unique.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="delba-de-oliveria" href="#delba-de-oliveria">
  </a>
  Delba De Oliveria
</h2>

<p><div class="ff-container ff-ready ff-active"><button type="button" aria-label="Pause animation playback" aria-pressed="false" class="c-btn gif-button fs-s gap-2"><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="crayons-icon gif-play"><path d="M12 22C6.477 22 2 17.523 2 12S6.477 2 12 2s10 4.477 10 10-4.477 10-10 10ZM10.622 8.415a.4.4 0 0 0-.622.332v6.506a.4.4 0 0 0 .622.332l4.879-3.252a.4.4 0 0 0 0-.666l-4.88-3.252h.001Z"></path></svg><svg width="24" height="24" viewBox="0 0 24 24" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="crayons-icon gif-pause"><path d="M12 22C6.477 22 2 17.523 2 12S6.477 2 12 2s10 4.477 10 10-4.477 10-10 10ZM9 9v6h2V9H9Zm4 0v6h2V9h-2Z"></path></svg>GIF</button>
<canvas class="ff-canvas ff-canvas-ready" width="678" height="479">
</canvas><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F8931ekj88c4fn23282wh.gif" alt="" loading="lazy" width="1284" height="908" data-animated="true" id="animated-0" class="ff-image"></div></p>

<p>Delba’s project-based portfolio is remarkable. She even made a <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?t=142&v=zl9iXZrw_dw&feature=youtu.be" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">YouTube video</a> about it!</p>

<p>Understandably, Delba has since updated <a href="https://delba.dev/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">her website</a> to be more focused on her blog, so the live version looks a bit different today (still looks great.) Make sure to check out the original, minimal-looking version by flicking through the video.</p>

<p><strong>🎨 Design tip</strong>:  Even though Delba’s website is fairly minimal, she sparks joy in the user with delightful highlighter animation using a JavaScript library called <a href="https://github.com/rough-stuff/rough-notation" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Rough Notation</a>.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="alex-pate" href="#alex-pate">
  </a>
  <a href="https://alexjpate.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Alex Pate</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fwjy6rjq4h51u69qjieth.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fwjy6rjq4h51u69qjieth.png" alt="Alex's portfolio in a desktop orientation" loading="lazy" width="800" height="870"></a><br>
<a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fz2q82i29k8r9l4177y0f.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fz2q82i29k8r9l4177y0f.png" alt="Alex's portfolio in a mobile orientation" loading="lazy" width="800" height="1364"></a></p>

<p>Notice how Alex uses a fairly narrow single-column layout and lists only a few menu items at the top. This removed the need to collapse columns or introduce a hamburger menu on mobile, allowing Alex to ship his website sooner.</p>

<p>The <strong>Selected Work</strong> section, though minimal, is beautifully designed and effective:</p>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fjbtggj8dt7c2e4grg247.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fjbtggj8dt7c2e4grg247.png" alt="The Selected Work section of Alex's portfolio" loading="lazy" width="800" height="768"></a></p>

<p>Alex understands someone looking at a portfolio is probably browsing and too many options is overwhelming. He makes it easy by showcasing only his 3 most recent works. For everything else, you can click <strong>View All Work →</strong>.</p>

<p><strong>🎨 Design tip</strong>: Through introducing borders in the project screenshots, Alex introduced a pop of colour, while also making the design breathable.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="lee-robinson" href="#lee-robinson">
  </a>
  <a href="https://leerob.io/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Lee Robinson</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fx8akine1zc3t9ixh9y6c.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fx8akine1zc3t9ixh9y6c.png" alt="Lee's website in light mode" loading="lazy" width="800" height="478"></a><br>
<a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fu9sfi91vj4kkr013kyhb.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fu9sfi91vj4kkr013kyhb.png" alt="Lee's website in dark mode" loading="lazy" width="800" height="478"></a></p>

<p><strong>🎨 Design tip:</strong> Because the  background and Lee’s photo are grey, the gradient really pops!</p>

<h2>
  <a name="franklina-amoah" href="#franklina-amoah">
  </a>
  <a href="https://www.franklinaamoah.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Franklina Amoah</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fksewfdh0tgfsqf4kf80w.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fksewfdh0tgfsqf4kf80w.png" alt="A screenshot of Franklina's portfolio, featuring her photo and bio." loading="lazy" width="800" height="435"></a></p>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fw8cgsb2o96yk36qlftlz.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fw8cgsb2o96yk36qlftlz.png" alt="" loading="lazy" width="800" height="439"></a></p>

<p>Notice how when you press <strong>View case study</strong>, Franklkina links to a PDF. This is extremely smart because it saves time and allowed Franklina to put her portfolio in front of employers sooner.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="brian-lovin" href="#brian-lovin">
  </a>
  <a href="https://brianlovin.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Brian Lovin</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F9iqdyeh53ld0ccolg0ay.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F9iqdyeh53ld0ccolg0ay.png" alt="A screenshot of Brian's Wiki-inspired minimal portfolio" loading="lazy" width="800" height="498"></a></p>

<p>Brian takes a novel approach to a personal website with this Wiki-style navigation.</p>

<p><strong>🎨 Design tip:</strong> Using an icon pack is a great way to introduce consistency regardless of your design skill.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="justin-chi" href="#justin-chi">
  </a>
  <a href="https://www.justinchi.me/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Justin Chi</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fqrt21nnislxw7s2ro5p4.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fqrt21nnislxw7s2ro5p4.png" alt="A screenshot of Justin's portfolio, featuring his information in a terminal in JavaScript notation" loading="lazy" width="800" height="508"></a></p>

<p>Smart idea to express your personal information in code!</p>

<p><strong>🎨 Design tip:</strong> Minimal doesn’t necessarily mean black and white. Justin uses a muted, pastel-y colour scheme to great effect.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="nick-mcmillian" href="#nick-mcmillian">
  </a>
  <a href="https://nick.computer/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Nick McMillian</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fzqqb42s93nwv0c94sgvb.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fzqqb42s93nwv0c94sgvb.png" alt="A screenshot of Nick's black and white portfolio with a pop of color" loading="lazy" width="800" height="544"></a></p>

<h2>
  <a name="darius-foroux" href="#darius-foroux">
  </a>
  <a href="https://dariusforoux.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Darius Foroux</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F5pkvghvqe2l4cr3h5b1w.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F5pkvghvqe2l4cr3h5b1w.png" alt="A screenshot of Darius's website" loading="lazy" width="800" height="480"></a></p>

<p>Ah, you got me! Darius is a writer, not a developer but his website is a great example of getting out of the way of the important stuff.</p>

<h2>
  <a name="josh-comeau" href="#josh-comeau">
  </a>
  Josh Comeau
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fot1qa6e56ddfa0iema99.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fot1qa6e56ddfa0iema99.png" alt="A screnshot of Josh's portfolio, featuring an illustration of Josh and his most recent posts" loading="lazy" width="800" height="480"></a></p>

<p>Josh’s portfolio is not quite as minimal as the others, but it deserves an honourable mention. After all, the idea for this post was sparked during our conversation on <a href="https://scrimba.com/podcast" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">The Scrimba Podcast</a>.</p>

<p>It has delightful interactions: </p>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fi9bw0wtf1zhlh3qtycr0.gif" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fi9bw0wtf1zhlh3qtycr0.gif" alt="A screen recording hearting one of Josh's posts and showing off the delightful animation" loading="lazy" width="800" height="565"></a></p>

<p>Josh wrote all about how he built his blog <a href="https://www.joshwcomeau.com/blog/how-i-built-my-blog/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">here</a>, and includes screenshots of his previous iterations, which aren’t quite as polished:</p>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F826e6hfhyddueaw8ej6s.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F826e6hfhyddueaw8ej6s.png" alt="An early iteration of Josh's portfolio, which is much simpler but equally effective" loading="lazy" width="800" height="478"></a></p>

<h2>
  <a name="coner-dewey" href="#coner-dewey">
  </a>
  <a href="https://www.conordewey.com/" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">Coner Dewey</a>
</h2>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F60hkkwpdb94yifgkny9a.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F60hkkwpdb94yifgkny9a.png" alt="A screenshot of Coner's homepage, featuring his picture and a short bio" loading="lazy" width="800" height="499"></a></p>

<p><a href="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fkm4fair8djl81xkspk03.png" class="article-body-image-wrapper"><img src="https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fkm4fair8djl81xkspk03.png" alt="A screenshot of Coner's about page, with more information about him" loading="lazy" width="800" height="498"></a></p>

<p>While Coner’s homepage puts an emphasis on blogs, the “recent posts” section could be repurposed to feature projects.</p>

        <div class="
            comment__body
            text-styles
            text-styles--secondary
            body
            
            
          ">
          <blockquote>
<p>With that said, to get a web developer job, you’ll need your portfolio to stand out and look good</p>
</blockquote>

<p>This is a total myth. You really do not need a portfolio at all. An interesting and active GitHub/GitLab/whatever account is a far better way to gauge someone as a developer. All of the best developers I've hired have not had portfolios. It's also interesting to note that of the candidates I've interviewed who <em>did</em> have a portfolio (by far the minority) - it actually counted against them in more than one case.</p>


        </div>

        <div class="
            comment__body
            text-styles
            text-styles--secondary
            body
            
            
          ">
          <p>You make a good point! I will try and find a different way to phrase it.</p>

<p>I believe a well-executed portfolio can help you showcase your projects, tell your story, make you more memorable, create talking points in an interview, and stand out.</p>

<p>Equally, if your portfolio is <em>too</em> scrappy, that can count against you. </p>

<p>As much as a Hiring Manager might try to remove their bias from the situation, it's hard not to hold a candidate's portfolio to the high standard they're going to expect when you contribute to their website.  </p>

<p>This was actually the motivation behind this post. </p>

<p>Oftentimes, we're <em>too</em> ambitious with our portfolios and ship something incomplete or otherwise less than ideal. That can bias a Hiring Manager and fundamentally get in the way of what really matters as a web developer, which is your experience, projects, and value proposition. </p>


        </div>

    </div>

<h2>  <a name="build-fast-on-mongodb-atlas-without-the-fear-of-outgrowing" href="#build-fast-on-mongodb-atlas-without-the-fear-of-outgrowing">  </a>  <a href="https://www.mongodb.com/cloud/atlas/lp/try3?utm_campaign=display_dev.to-broad_pl_flighted_atlas_tryatlaslp_prosp_gic-null_ww-all_dev_dv-all_eng_leadgen&utm_source=dev.to&utm_medium=display&utm_content=prototype&bb=263133">Build fast on MongoDB Atlas without the fear of outgrowing.</a>
</h2>
<p>Don't let your database dictate your speed. With MongoDB Atlas, the same document model you use for your MVP handles global scale across AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud. Start free and stay fast as you grow.</p>
<p><a href="https://www.mongodb.com/cloud/atlas/lp/try3?utm_campaign=display_dev.to-broad_pl_flighted_atlas_tryatlaslp_prosp_gic-null_ww-all_dev_dv-all_eng_leadgen&utm_source=dev.to&utm_medium=display&utm_content=prototype&bb=263133" class="crayons-btn crayons-btn--primary" role="button">Start Free</a></p>

    <script>
  var userSignedIn = false;
  if (document.readyState === 'complete' || document.readyState === 'interactive') {
    initAuth();
  } else {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', initAuth);
  }

  function initAuth() {
    var paramToken = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search).get('jwt');

    if (paramToken && !userSignedIn) {
      authenticateUser(paramToken);
    } else {
      var iframe = document.createElement('iframe');
      iframe.style.display = 'none';
      iframe.src = 'https://forem.com/auth_pass/iframe';
  
      document.body.appendChild(iframe);
  
      window.addEventListener('message', function(event) {
        if (event.origin !== 'https://forem.com' && event.origin !== window.location.origin) {
          return;
        }
  
        var data = event.data;
  
        if (data.authenticated && !userSignedIn) {
          authenticateUser(data.token);
        } else if(data.authenticated && window.ReactNativeWebView && window.ReactNativeWebView.postMessage) {
          window.ReactNativeWebView.postMessage(JSON.stringify({
            action: 'login',
            token: data.token,
          }));
        }
      });  
    }

    function authenticateUser(token) {
      fetch('/auth_pass/token_login', {
        method: 'POST',
        credentials: 'include',
        headers: {
          'Content-Type': 'application/json',
          'X-CSRF-Token': getMetaContent('csrf-token'),
        },
        body: JSON.stringify({ token: token }),
      })
      .then(function(response) {
        return response.json();
      })
      .then(function(data) {
        if (data.success) {
          if (document.head.querySelector('meta[name="user-signed-in"][content="false"]')) {
            // Reload the page to update the user's state
            location.reload();
          }
        }
      })
      .catch(function(error) {
        console.error('Error during authentication:', error);
      });
    }

    function getMetaContent(name) {
      var element = document.querySelector('meta[name="' + name + '"]');
      return element ? element.getAttribute('content') : '';
    }
  }
</script>

  
  
  
  
  
<iframe src="https://forem.com/auth_pass/iframe" style="display: none;"></iframe></body></html>
